TextENGNew: ...Disposing of the body.
The ultimate way to prevent a 'setup'... was to ensure the body was never discovered.
It was even more important than the killing itself.
Thinking broadly... the first thing that came to mind was the swamp.
...Onigafuchi—the dreaded, fathomless swamp that appeared in the legends of Onigafuchi Village and was revered and feared to this day.
Yes, the swamp called Onigafuchi had come first... and the village had taken its name from it.@k...This swamp was the very origin of Onigafuchi Village.
A bottomless swamp—once you sank beneath its surface, no one, no matter who, could ever rise again... all were swallowed into the realm of demons deep below.@kOr so the legend went.
If I was going to mimic Oyashiro-sama's curse, disposing of the body and weapon in this swamp seemed almost textbook.
But... they called it bottomless, yet no one actually knew what it was really like.
changeReason: New translation alters negation, potentially changing the core assertion.

TextENGNew: And they say that large creatures like humans produce enormous amounts of gas as they decompose, generating enough buoyancy to float back to the surface.@kA few weights wouldn't be enough to counteract that.
...Come to think of it, in the curse from two years ago, Rika-chan's mother had supposedly drowned herself in this swamp.
...And the ringleader who disappeared in the very first incident was rumored to have accidentally drowned while trying to dump a body here.
...I'd never heard of those corpses floating back up or being found.
...Then... shouldn't I dispose of the body here too...?
...........................I could discard the weapon here, at least.
...And if, for instance, the uncle was ambushed while out on his motorcycle... this would be a convenient place to dump the bike, too.
(I knew that Satoko's uncle primarily used a motorcycle for transportation.)
But... the body...
After much deliberation... I decided not to dispose of the body in the swamp.
...Both the drowning suicide and the accidental death were just rumors... No one had actually confirmed either one.
There were plenty of rumors that the suicide was staged, and the killer might not have drowned at all—he could still be on the run to this day.
...In other words... no one had ever verified that a corpse thrown into this swamp would stay submerged.
Then what would I do with the body...?
A method to hide it from sight forever, where no one would ever find it...
changeReason: Old contains content about dismemberment that is not present in this line's JPN (人目につかないよう、永遠に隠し通す方法は = 'a method to hide it from sight forever'). Text appears misaligned.

TextENGNew: ...Following that line of reasoning to its conclusion... I arrived at the orthodox answer: bury it in a hole.
The question of where to bury it... was inseparable from the question of where to kill him.
Obviously, the less time and distance I had to spend with the body, the better.
With that in mind, it would be ideal to dig the hole in advance, and to have it as close to the crime scene as possible.
...The selection of the crime scene... required the utmost caution and thoroughness.
A place where no one would witness anything... with a spot to hide and launch a surprise attack... and where I could dig a body disposal hole nearby.
...I ran through every possible destination the uncle might head to from Satoko's house.
When I reconstructed a mental map of the surrounding area... I found a location that met every condition with almost unbelievable ease.
...A small back road cutting through the woods.
Unless the uncle took it into his head to venture deep into the mountains toward Yagouchi... this was a path he'd almost certainly pass through on his way anywhere.
And this back road was only used by Satoko's household and a handful of neighboring houses.
...The number of people who used this path was ideally... few.
In these woods... I would lie in wait.
...Was an ambush actually feasible here?
I stepped into the trees and tested various hiding spots.@k...Extremely quiet.@k...I could sharpen my awareness to the absolute maximum here.
Here, I would wait... for however long it took... for Satoko's uncle to pass by.
...I'd actually hesitated over this somewhat.
The question of whether I should lure him here by some means.
...Whether there was some risk in luring him.
...That man made Satoko handle all the shopping and errands.
...He rarely left the house on his own.
...Without some kind of scheme, he might never come outside.@k...That was the concern.
Tomorrow was the Watanagashi festival.
...Would he go out for the festival, or would he stay inside?
If he stayed inside... how would I drag him out of his house?
...That's right.
...Would he... let Satoko go to the festival?
Satoko would go to the festival.@k...Meanwhile, I would call the man.
...This is the Okinomiya police station.@kWe've taken your niece into protective custody. Could you come pick her up immediately?
...It didn't even have to be the police.
'This is the clinic. The girl from your house has been injured.@kPlease come pick her up.' That would work too.
...If I claimed to be the police or a hospital, told him to come, and hung up—he'd rush over to find out what was going on.
And just the other day, he'd been visited by people from the consultation center.
...He wouldn't dare ignore a summons about his niece now.
...For a man with absolutely zero ability to fend for himself... Satoko was indispensable to his daily life.
Conclusion:
...If he went out for the festival... I would ambush him here, on his way there or back.
If he didn't... I would lure him out with a phone call.
If the phone lure was the plan, I first needed to ensure that Satoko had left for the festival and was away from the house.
...It would be convenient to have Mion take Satoko to the festival.
...She'd mentioned that her aunt was the local welfare commissioner.
...If I pressed Mion hard to bring Satoko to the festival no matter what... knowing her, she'd leverage that connection and find a way to get Satoko there.
...Satoko should just... take a breather and enjoy the festival with everyone.
...She should have fun, reminisce about the peaceful days... and by the time she headed home, it would all be over.@k...Yes... that would be best.
With that decided... I needed to dig a hole somewhere in these woods to hide the body.
...Inconspicuous. Undiscoverable.
...A place where absolutely no one could find me during the vulnerable time of burying the body.
...I'd intended to dig close to the crime scene... but I naturally drifted farther away.
Deep in the bosom of the black forest.
...Only the higurashi cicadas' voices echoed here, warning that this was no place for people to tread lightly.
Digging a hole here, where the ground was densely threaded with roots and undergrowth... proved far more difficult than I'd imagined.
...The small gardening trowel I'd brought hidden on my person... was completely inadequate.
Still, as I explored the area, I managed to find a spot where the digging was feasible.
...Tomorrow, I'd bring a proper shovel from the storage shed at home.
A shovel would do the job.
How big of a hole did you actually need to bury a person?
...It would probably need to be quite deep.
...But if I cut corners here, I'd be allowing a 'setup' to occur.
...That was absolutely unacceptable.
...Spend as much time as you need.@k...Do not skimp on the effort required to make him vanish completely...
I checked my wristwatch.@k...It was a little past evening.
I'd probably accomplished all I could here today.
...I really wanted to dig the hole tonight, even after dark, but sneaking out at night would make Mom suspicious.@k...I couldn't afford to arouse my parents' suspicion.
I needed to get home and call Mion's house.
I had to get her to secure a promise from Satoko tonight.@k...And with that, today would be done.
...Done.@k...Really?
...Was this truly everything I could do to prepare right now...?
...I'd decided where to kill him.
...I'd decided how to kill him.
I'd decided, of course, how to dispose of the body.
...I hadn't decided on a time, but that would depend on circumstances.@kThere was no way to predetermine it.
...Was that really everything?@kWas this really going to work?@kWas there anything I hadn't accounted for?@kWould everything really go according to plan?
Now that the time was approaching, anxieties welled up one after another.
...It was only natural to be anxious.@k...This would be... the first and last... great undertaking of my life.
Failure was not an option... and being my first time, I had no experience.
I had no know-how, no knack.@k...So of course I'd be anxious...
The black clouds of doubt... even offered the utterly pathetic suggestion that doing nothing would be the safest course of action...
...Have you already forgotten how pitiful Satoko looked?!
...On the surface, I put on a display of fierce determination... but deep down, my knees were rattling.
...I'd never even won a real fight... Could I actually kill a person...?
Ambush or not... the opponent was a large, much older man.@kHe had a menacing face and looked well accustomed to violence.
...Could it be...
...I might have every angle before and after the murder covered...@kbut wasn't it the actual killing—the most critical part—that I was most uncertain about?
All the preparation, all the meticulous concealment... would be meaningless if the murder itself failed.
...Damn it...!
After coming this far... don't be pathetic, Keiichi Maebara!
I could see right through the ploy—this was just my fear trying to make me hesitate.
changeReason: Old translation frames this as addressing another person ('your ulterior motive') while new correctly presents it as self-directed internal monologue ('my fear trying to make me hesitate'), matching JPN 俺に二の足を踏ませよう.

TextENGNew: ...Let's go home... Go home and steady my mind.
Tomorrow... would be a turning point in my life... one that would dwarf anything like going to college, getting a job, marriage, or having children...
...A day of murder... killing someone... for someone else's sake.
Once that day was behind us... we would reclaim it all.
...Those tranquil, joyful days that felt like an endless Indian summer—days we never imagined could end.
I drove my pedals hard, heading home.
...I felt somehow weightless.
...As though the gears of my body and soul had slipped out of alignment.@k...A subtle disconnect between where my hands and feet actually were, and where I felt them to be.
My vision felt distant and narrow.
...I was staking the rest of my life on this monumental undertaking, and yet... it felt strangely distant, as if it were happening to someone else.@k...An unreal sensation.@kWhat was the word for it?
...Fine.
...Let the anxiety soak through you, Keiichi Maebara.
...If it makes you cautious, meticulous, and even more careful in your actions...@kthen that feeling is actually vital.
In the end, that sensation... never subsided, not once that entire night.
It was the first time I'd ever called Mion's house.
I looked up her number in the class phone directory... and dialed the absurdly short number typical of rural areas...
It was dinnertime.
She was probably home.
...Just as I was starting to think nobody was going to answer...@k@|@ythe receiver was finally picked up.
"...Hello, Sonozaki residence."
"...........................Oh, I'm sorry for calling so late.@k@vS03/01/140100573. ...Um... is this Mion?"
"...Who is this?"
"It's me... Keiichi."
"Oh... ah, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300285. What's up? Calling at this hour."
Her speech sounded strangely thick, somehow slurred.
"Sorry for the late call.@k@vS03/01/140100576. ...Do you have a minute?"
"Sure. ...What is it?"
"It's about Satoko."
I heard her draw a sharp, quiet breath on the other end.
"...Satoko... things have gotten really bad for her, haven't they?@k@vS03/03/140300288. ...I hope she's okay..."
Whatever haze or drowsiness had been in Mion's voice vanished completely...
changeReason: New translation adds mion, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "Of course she's not okay.@k@vS03/01/140100579. ...You saw it too, didn't you?@k@vS03/01/140100580. ...The state Satoko was in today."
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...We've all been thinking desperately about how to help."
"But in the end, nobody came up with anything... and we didn't save Satoko.@k@vS03/01/140100584. ...In the end, we're all just standing by, watching."
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...............I guess... you're right.@k@vS03/03/140300293. ...Maybe all I did in the end was watch..."
"...So here's the thing.@k@vS03/01/140100586. At the very least... couldn't we give her one night to have fun?"
"Huh? Have fun...?"

"...She's been beaten down so badly lately, you can see it...@k@vS03/01/140100588. So I was thinking.@k@vS03/01/140100589. ...At the very least, let's bring Satoko to the festival tomorrow.@k@vS03/01/140100590. ...Let her forget all the bad stuff and enjoy herself for one night."
changeReason: New translation adds satoko; removes watanagashi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "Why...?@k@vS03/01/140100591. ...I just want to give her a break on the night of the festival, at least.@k@vS03/01/140100592. ...What's wrong with that?"
"...Why what?"
I was having trouble understanding what Mion was getting at...
"...Why...... well...@k@vS03/03/140300299. ...Hmm! ...W-Why don't you take her yourself, Kei-chan?"
Mion was being... weirdly persistent about this.@k...No... more like... perceptive.

"Actually, I have something I need to do tomorrow.@k@vS03/01/140100595. ...I can't make it to the festival.@k@vS03/01/140100596. I'll probably be free by evening, but... I might not be able to join up with you guys."
"...Something to do...?"
"It's nothing major.@k@vS03/01/140100598. I'll probably finish quickly, but... there's a chance I won't be able to meet up with everyone tomorrow."
"Hey, Mion.@k@vS03/01/140100600. ...Could I leave Satoko to you... just for tomorrow night...?"
Mion took a long time to answer.@k...Something about her had been off for a while now.@k...As though her emotions were running high.
Huh...?@kWas that a sob?@k...Mion...?!
...Are you... crying...?
"...........I... I said no... I don't want to...@k@vS03/03/140300306. Don't just... dump her on me...@k@vS03/03/140300307. ...and then disappear..."
"...I-I'm not trying to dump her on you.@k@vS03/01/140100602. ...It's just for tomorrow night."
"But that was a lie!!"
...What...?@k...Mion... what was she... talking about...?
"You said it was just for the night of Watanagashi...@k@vS03/03/140300309. ...But in the end... it was forever...@k@vS03/03/140300310. ...You never came back..."
"...I couldn't... I couldn't keep the promise I made to you...@k@vS03/03/140300315. ...I left that girl all alone..."
...I had no idea what to say.
...Mion was clearly... no longer talking to me.
...Who she was talking to... I didn't know.
All I could do... was listen in stunned silence.
"...You never... came back after that... and now you're calling again... just to dump that girl on me one more time..."
"...Mi-... Mion... what are you talking about...?"
...The moment I said her name... whatever had taken hold of Mion seemed to break...

"...Ah... ahahahahahahahahaha...@k@vS03/03/140300321. ...I-... I'm sorry...@k@vS03/03/140300322. ...Did I say something weird just now...?@k@vS03/03/140300323. ...I must have dozed off... I was drinking too much earlier... ahahaha..."
"...Was that... from Satoshi...?"
...Mion didn't answer.
...All I could hear were muffled sobs coming through the receiver.

"S-Sorry.@k@vS03/03/140300325. ...I think I'm still half-asleep.@k@vS03/03/140300326. ...I was napping and dreaming, and your call woke me up...@k@vS03/03/140300327. ...That's all, that's all. Just a dream."
...I had no idea how it had come about.
...But I was fairly certain that last year, on this very day...
...Satoshi had called Mion.@k...And told her: take Satoko to the festival for me.
And when she asked why he couldn't take her himself...@khe'd answered the same way I had.
"...Satoshi...... didn't go to the festival... did he?"
"...........Yeah.@k@vS03/03/140300329. ...He said he had something to do and couldn't go.@k@vS03/03/140300330. ...I took Satoko-chan to the festival in his place."
"...At the time... Satoshi said it too, didn't he?@k@vS03/01/140100607. ...'I'm leaving Satoko to you.'"

"...............Yeah.@k@vS03/03/140300332. ...He did say that.@k@vS03/03/140300333. Just for tomorrow night, he asked.@k@vS03/03/140300334. ...The way you put it... was so very similar to how he did... Ahahaha.@k@vS03/03/140300335. ...I just got a little emotional, remembering. Sorry about that."
changeReason: Both say Mion confirms Satoshi said 'just for tomorrow night' and notes the similarity - same content.

TextENGNew: I hadn't expected this.@k...Satoshi had made the exact same phone call I had, one year ago today.
...After that, Mion had continued.
...He was lying, she said.@k...He said it would only be for the night of the festival, she said...
"...........I see.@k@vS03/01/140100609. ...A few days later... Satoshi... vanished."
'Vanished' was perhaps a slightly vague way to put it.
...Whether it was running away or something else entirely... Satoshi had abandoned Satoko and disappeared.
"...........Yes.@k@vS03/03/140300337. ...He didn't transfer schools. He didn't run away from home. ...He simply... vanished."
...At that moment... an idea more indistinct than mist drifted through my mind.
Satoshi had made the exact same phone call as me, one year ago.
...Why... would Satoshi have made the exact same call...?
If... in the truest, most literal sense... he had called for the exact same reason as me...@k...then last year's murder of the aunt was...........
...Last year.
...Satoko had been relentlessly abused by her uncle and aunt for so long.
...The aunt, who had been especially cruel to Satoko...@k...on that night, cloaked in the name of Oyashiro-sama's curse...@k...he had put her in the ground.
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...If I thought about it that way... everything fell into place perfectly.
"...Last year, Satoko's aunt was beaten to death.@k@vS03/01/140100612. ...That was... Satoshi... wasn't it?"
...No, but... that would mean...
...But... Satoshi was supposed to be a coward who abandoned Satoko and ran away...?!
The idea that Satoshi... had resolved to kill someone... to save Satoko... could that really be...
"...........I... actually considered that possibility once, too.@k@vS03/03/140300341. ...I feel bad saying it about Satoshi, but..."
...A few days later... Satoshi vanished.@kOn Satoko's birthday.
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: When I'd first heard that, I'd been furious—what a cruel day to choose to run away.
...But...@klooked at this way...@kthe story became entirely different...
Satoshi... had probably been even less composed than I was now.
...As her biological brother, watching his little sister suffer day after day right before his eyes... perhaps he couldn't have stayed calm.
...And that was why... the aunt's battered corpse had been discovered so easily, that very night.
Satoshi had lost himself in rage and hadn't hidden the body.
He had permitted the 'setup' to occur.
If the police had launched a full investigation... it was easy to imagine that it wouldn't have taken long to narrow the suspect down to Satoshi.
Satoshi... had wished for peaceful days with Satoko, and had briefly tasted them...@k...but was gradually, inexorably cornered.
And then... he'd tried to hold out at least until Satoko's birthday... but finally... the noose had tightened completely.
...At the time, Satoshi had been carrying the savings he'd painstakingly accumulated to buy a large stuffed animal as Satoko's birthday present.
He could buy the stuffed animal and give it to her... and then be arrested.
...Or he could use the money... and vanish. That was the choice he'd been forced to make.
...And Satoshi... chose not to make Satoko the sister of a murderer...
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...If that was truly how it happened... what an agonizing choice it must have been.
...All that money he'd saved to see Satoko's face light up... he'd had to spend it to make her cry instead.
...And so he used the savings...@kand vanished... to Tokyo, as the rumors said.
"...............Satoshi... was that how... he disappeared..."
"...I don't... know.@k@vS03/03/140300343. ...Ooishi seems to think so, though."
Ooishi...@k...Had that unpleasant man hounded Satoshi relentlessly... slowly driving him into a corner...?
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "The circumstantial evidence all lines up, but... I think your theory is probably wrong, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300345. ...Because an unrelated person confessed to the crime, and the case was closed."
changeReason: New translation adds kei-chan, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...That's right.@k...Some deviant had confessed... and the case was settled.
"They said he was just a random madman with no connection to Hinamizawa at all.@k@vS03/03/140300347. Of course, that means Satoshi had nothing to do with it either."
...You couldn't just assume that so easily.
...You never knew where or how people were connected to each other...
...If that man had taken the blame... then it was a perfect crime.
...Though if it truly was a perfect crime... then Satoshi would have had no reason to disappear.
"Besides... I don't think Satoshi had the kind of money to fund an escape.@k@vS03/03/140300350. The day Satoshi withdrew his savings—"
"...Nobody believes me when I tell them this, but...@k@vS03/03/140300352. He actually used the money to buy the stuffed animal he'd promised Satoko."
"...Hold on.@k@vS03/01/140100615. ...If he didn't use the money to flee, and instead bought the stuffed animal... then he would have had no escape funds at all."
changeReason: New translation removes satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...Yeah.@k@vS03/03/140300355. ...That's why... it stops making sense.@k@vS03/03/140300356. ...If Satoshi killed her aunt, why did he use his escape funds to buy a stuffed animal?@k@vS03/03/140300357. ...If he didn't kill her, why did he disappear?"
changeReason: New translation adds satoshi, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...I didn't get it.
...The more I learned... the less I understood the man named Satoshi.
The two of us... fell silent.
...I couldn't even remember how we'd gotten onto this topic.
"Ahahahahaha... sorry.@k@vS03/03/140300359. ...I think we got way off track.@k@vS03/03/140300360. ...Oh, but about taking Satoko to the festival—you don't need to worry about that."
"...When everyone went to the shrine to help set up for the festival, we started talking about inviting Satoko.@k@vS03/03/140300362. I went and talked to her uncle."

"He was cold about it and basically said 'do whatever you want,' but he gave the okay.@k@vS03/03/140300365. ...So Satoko's coming to the festival tomorrow.@k@vS03/03/140300366. ...You don't have to worry, Kei-chan.@k@vS03/03/140300367. ...Satoshi."
changeReason: New translation adds kei-chan, satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...Stupid... I keep telling you, I'm not Satoshi..."
I felt a little relieved.
...Even without my phone call... Satoko had already been invited to the festival.
Not as some blood-soaked scheme to leave the uncle alone so I could kill him, but rather...@k...a genuinely pure invitation, born from everyone simply wanting Satoko to have fun.
The end result was the same... but that small difference meant everything to me.
"...Sorry.@k@vS03/01/140100622. ...I guess I didn't need to call after all.@k@vS03/01/140100623. ...I should have just left it to you from the start."
"...No, I'm the one who should apologize.@k@vS03/03/140300369. ...I completely lost my composure, and that's not like me.@k@vS03/03/140300370. ...Sorry if I said anything weird."
For a while, we kept apologizing to each other.
"Well, I'll let you go now.@k@vS03/01/140100626. ...Good night."
"...What was the thing you had to do?"
...As if cutting into our parting words... Mion struck a nerve.
"...Or is it like with Satoshi... where you can't say?"
I deflected her question... with an answer that wasn't really an answer.
...Mion didn't press me any further after that.
"Okay, this time for real... good night."

"...I don't know why you're giving me the exact same phone call Satoshi-kun did, but...@k@vS03/03/140300376. ...I'm going to keep the promise this time.@k@vS03/03/140300377. ...I will look after Satoko-chan."
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, satoko-chan, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: "...........I'm hanging up now."
...Click.
Satoshi Houjou.
...Just who... were you...?
...I had scorned you as a coward who abandoned his sister and fled.
I'd always believed... you had no right to be called Nii-nii.
But...... now I didn't know what to think.
Oyashiro-sama's curse in the fourth year.@k...The aunt bludgeoned to death... and Satoshi's disappearance.
...And now... me, about to carry out the fifth year of Oyashiro-sama's curse.
By strange fate, my actions... on the eve of the curse... had aligned perfectly with Satoshi's.
No, that wasn't all...
...If this all began with Satoko suffering and being made miserable... then for days, weeks before this... Satoshi and I had been walking the same path.
Just now, when I spoke with Mion... she had asked if I was Satoshi.
...If a third party like Mion's first impression was to think that... then it must have been true.
Then, just as Satoshi had accomplished... I would succeed in the murder.
But this was where our overlap ended.
I was far calmer than Satoshi... and far more calculating.
...The more heated things became... the cooler I actually grew.
...That was why I would not repeat Satoshi's mistakes.
I would cleanly erase that man from the world... and reclaim our peaceful days.

...Perhaps Satoshi had been with me ever since I chose his bat as my weapon.@k...No, maybe even before that.@k...From the moment I decided to become Satoko's Nii-nii... Satoshi and I had been walking side by side.
changeReason: New translation adds satoko, altering character/location references.

TextENGNew: ...Satoshi.@k...Were you truly... a coward?
...Or were you... the real Nii-nii that Satoko still reveres to this day... worthy of that love...?
I'd never met him, never spoken to him... didn't even know his face well... and yet, the depth of kinship I felt toward him... was unlike anything I'd ever experienced.
